title: Dc/dc resonant converters and power factor correction using resonant converters, and corresponding control methods
abstract: Various improvements are provided to resonant DC/DC and AC/DC converter circuit. The improvements are of particular interest for LLC circuits. Some examples relate to self-oscillating circuit and others relate to converter circuits with frequency control, for example for power factor correction, driven by an oscillator.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. An AC/DC PFC converter, comprising: an AC input for providing an input voltage and an input current to the input of a rectifier (12), wherein the rectifier (12) is arranged for rectifying the input voltage to obtain a rectified input voltage; a half bridge inverter for receiving the rectified input voltage and comprising a first switch (28) and a second switch (30), wherein a first output is defined from a node between the switches; an LLC circuit (20,24,26,27) coupled to the first output and having a second output for supplying a converter output voltage and current, wherein a voltage across a capacitor of the LLC circuit is provided as an electrical feedback parameter, wherein the electrical feedback parameter is representative of the input current, wherein the capacitor is arranged for acting as a resonance capacitor; and a control circuit for generating a gate drive signal for controlling the switching of the first switch and the second switch in dependence on the electrical feedback parameter, wherein a high gate drive signal turns on one switch and turns off the other switch and a low gate drive signal turns off the one switch and turns on the other switch, wherein the control circuit comprises an outer control loop (64') for setting a threshold level for the electrical feedback parameter in dependence on the converter output voltage or current and the rectified input voltage without measurement of the input current, and an inner control loop (62') for comparing the electrical feedback parameter with the threshold to derive the gate drive signal and to provide power factor correction. 2. The circuit as claimed in claim 1, wherein the inner control loop (62') is for comparing the voltage across the capacitor with the threshold at a switching instant of the gate drive signal. 3. The circuit as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the inner control loop (62') comprises an oscillator (208), and the inner control loop controls the oscillation frequency. 4. The circuit as claimed in any one of claim 1 or 2, wherein the inner control loop comprises a latch (72), wherein the timing of the latch switching is dependent on the electrical feedback parameter. 5. The circuit as claimed in claim 4, wherein the latch (72) comprises a flip flop, wherein one of the set and reset timings is controlled in dependence on the electrical feedback parameter and the other of the set and reset timings is controlled based on a fixed delay. 6. The circuit as claimed in any preceding claim, further comprising a correction unit (270,280,290) for modifying the relationship between the threshold level for the electrical feedback parameter and the rectified input voltage to the circuit. 7. The circuit as claimed in claim 6, wherein the correction unit is for: modifying the threshold level for the electrical feedback parameter in dependence on the rectified input voltage before the threshold level is compared with the electrical feedback parameter; modifying the rectified input voltage before supplying it to the outer control loop; or modifying the measured electrical feedback parameter before comparing it with the threshold. 8. The circuit as claimed in claim 6 or 7, wherein the relationship between the threshold level for the electrical feedback parameter and the rectified input voltage to the circuit comprises a function with the same ratio between the threshold level and the rectified input voltage for the minimum and maximum rectified input voltage, and a larger ratio for a mid-range rectified input voltage. 9. An LED driver, comprising: a power factor correction circuit as claimed in any preceding claim; and an isolation transformer at the output of the self-oscillating LLC circuit, wherein the secondary side of the isolation transformer is for driving an LED load. 10. An AC/DC PFC conversion method, comprising: rectifying an AC input; operating a half bridge inverter comprising a first switch and a second switch using a gate drive signal and providing an output from a node between the switches, wherein a high gate drive signal turns on one switch and turns off the other and a low gate drive signal turns off the one switch and turns on the other switch; providing a voltage across a capacitor of an LLC circuit as an electrical feedback parameter which is representative of the input current; controlling the switching of the first switch and the second switch in dependence on the electrical feedback parameter, by: setting a threshold level for the electrical feedback parameter in an outer control loop in dependence on the converter output voltage or current and the rectified input voltage without measurement of the input current; and comparing the electrical feedback parameter with the threshold in an inner control loop to derive the gate drive signal. 1 1. The method as claimed in claim 10, wherein the method comprises, in the inner control loop, comparing the voltage across the capacitor with the threshold at a switching instant of the gate drive signal. 12. The method as claimed in claim 10 or 1 1, comprising using the inner control loop to controls an oscillator and thereby control the oscillation frequency of the gate drive signal. 13. The method as claimed in any one of claims 10 to 12, comprising using the inner control loop to control the timing of the switching of a flip flop, by controlling one of the set and reset timings in dependence on the electrical feedback parameter and by controlling the other of the set and reset timings based on a fixed delay. 14. The method as claimed in any one of claims 10 to 13, further comprising modifying the relationship between the threshold level for the electrical feedback parameter and the rectified input voltage to the circuit, by: modifying the threshold level for the electrical feedback parameter in dependence on the rectified input voltage before the threshold level is compared with the 

 electrical feedback parameter; modifying the rectified input voltage before supplying it to the outer control loop; or modifying the measured electrical feedback parameter before comparing it with the threshold. 15. An LED driving method comprising providing conversion using the method of any one of claims 1 1 to 14, and driving an LED load using the converted DC voltage.
